content.Text:
[Wed 2026-04-22 00:00 PDT] Add a new "Smart Conversation Coach" module to this browser-based AI chat app. This is a JavaScript single-file app using the revealing module pattern (IIFEs) in app.js.

## What to Build

A `ConversationCoach` module that autonomously monitors conversation patterns and proactively offers coaching tips to help users write better prompts and have more effective conversations.

## How It Works

1. **Pattern Detection** - After each user message, analyze it for common issues:
   - Too vague/short prompts (under 15 chars with no context)
   - Missing context (no specifics, just "help me with X")  
   - Repeated questions (asking same thing differently)
   - Long unstructured prompts (wall of text over 500 chars with no line breaks)
   - Not providing examples when asking for formatting
   - Not specifying output format
   - Consecutive failed/error responses

2. **Coaching Tips** - Show non-intrusive tip badges below the chat input:
   - Appear as a small dismissible bar with an icon (🎯) and short tip text
   - Auto-dismiss after 8 seconds or on click
   - Tips should be genuinely helpful, not annoying
   - Max 1 tip per 3 messages (don't overwhelm)
   - Track dismissed tips so you don't repeat the same tip type for 10 messages

3. **Coach Dashboard** (Alt+Shift+H) - A panel showing:
   - Prompt quality score (0-100) based on recent messages
   - Top 3 improvement suggestions
   - Stats: avg prompt length, specificity score, success rate estimate
   - Streak counter for "good prompts" (ones that got long, substantive responses)
   - Toggle to enable/disable coaching

4. **Proactive Insights** - After every 10 user messages, auto-generate a brief "conversation health" summary noting patterns and improvements.

## Implementation Details

- Add the module as a new IIFE `const ConversationCoach = (() => { ... })();` at the END of app.js
- Follow the exact same pattern as other modules (revealing module pattern)
- Use `SafeStorage` for persistence (coach settings, tip history, stats)
- Register keyboard shortcut Alt+Shift+H via `KeyboardShortcuts.register` if available
- Register command via `CommandPalette.register` if available  
- Register slash command `/coach` via `SlashCommands.register` if available
- Hook into chat-output via MutationObserver to detect new messages
- The tip bar should be styled inline (create elements with JS, set style properties)
- Color scheme: use CSS variables if available, otherwise dark-friendly defaults

## Style Guidelines
- Tip bar: fixed at bottom of chat area, subtle background (rgba(59,130,246,0.1)), rounded corners, small font
- Dashboard: overlay panel similar to other panels in the app
- Keep it lightweight - no external dependencies

## Don't forget:
- Add CSS for the coach dashboard panel and tip bar to style.css
- Add a coach button to the toolbar area in index.html (🎯 icon) 
- Verify with `node -c app.js` that syntax is valid before finishing
I’m inspecting the app structure first so I can match the existing IIFE/module conventions and find the right integration points for storage, shortcuts, commands, and the toolbar.

ToolUse.name: Run rg -n "const .* = \(\(\) =>|SafeStorage|KeyboardShortcuts|CommandPalette|SlashCommands|MutationObserver|toolbar|chat-output|chat input|textarea|input" app.js index.html style.css

content.Text: `rg` isn’t installed here, so I’m switching to PowerShell text search and pulling the relevant sections from `app.js`, `index.html`, and `style.css` before wiring anything in.
